title: protocol.rs relay-facing String fields unbounded
description: Structs deserialized from relay messages (SetNicknameParams.nickname, UserNicknameParams.nickname, GroupCreateParams.name, SubscribeInvoiceResult.address/invoice_id/expires_at, GroupInfo.name/created_at, DeliverParams.from, WhisperDeliverParams.from, etc.) are plain String with no size cap. A rogue or compromised relay sends a multi-megabyte nickname or group name; the client allocates without limit before any handler runs. ClearMessage fields were hardened in previous rounds; the relay-facing protocol.rs structs were not. These are lower priority than ClearMessage because the relay is chosen by the user, but a compromised relay is a real threat model concern. Fix: add bounded deserializers mirroring the ClearMessage pattern for all String fields in protocol.rs structs.
